title: ruppia cirrhosa subsp. occidentalis
intro: Ruppia cirrhosa subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western marsh-dwarf, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Ruppiaceae. It is a perennial, rhizomatous herb that has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a potential treatment for conditions like arthritis and wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical application, and it can also be dried and used in herbal capsules or tinctures for its medicinal properties.
